task_completion.description: Load the FZK-Haus architectural model in Bonsai (BlenderBIM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender interface is visible with the Bonsai add-on active, and the FZK-Haus model is loaded, visible in the 3D viewport or the Outliner.

task_completion.description: Create an IfcApproval entity with the Status 'APPROVED'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai Project Overview or Project Information panel shows an IfcApproval entity created with the Status field explicitly set to 'APPROVED'.

task_completion.description: Create an IfcApproval entity with the Status 'PENDING' or 'REJECTED'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai Project Overview or Project Information panel shows a second IfcApproval entity created with the Status field explicitly set to either 'PENDING' or 'REJECTED'.

task_completion.description: Assign the 'APPROVED' approval to all IfcSlab elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: IfcSlab elements (4 entities) are selected in the Outliner or 3D viewport, and the Object Properties > IFC Approvals panel shows the 'APPROVED' approval being assigned to them.

task_completion.description: Assign the 'PENDING' or 'REJECTED' approval to all IfcWindow elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: IfcWindow elements (11 entities) are selected in the Outliner or 3D viewport, and the Object Properties > IFC Approvals panel shows the 'PENDING' or 'REJECTED' approval being assigned to them.
The Blender file save dialog or Bonsai export interface is visible, showing the target path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_element_approvals.ifc'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's graphical interface, panels, and outliner, rather than just executing Python scripts or terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual IFC elements in the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner shows the genuine IFC hierarchy (IfcProject, IfcSite, IfcBuilding, etc.) and the correct element types (IfcSlab, IfcWindow) are highlighted/selected.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's save/export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender file browser or Bonsai export UI is used to save the file, rather than a terminal command writing a mock file to the destination.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FZK-Haus model contains exactly 4 IfcSlab entities and 11 IfcWindow entities. The final output must be saved to the exact path: /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_element_approvals.ifc.
